dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8654 IH: Afterschool for All Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Goldman of New York (for himself, Mr. Gomez, Mr. Kennedy of New York, Mr. Latimer, Mr. Moulton, Mr. Carson, Mr. Cisneros, Mr. Fields, Mrs. Beatty, Mr. Cohen, and Ms. Norton) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to reauthorize the Nita M. Lowey Community Learning Centers program for fiscal years 2026 through 2035, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Afterschool for All Act.

section.header: Expanding the Community Learning Centers program

subsection.text: Section 4206 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 7176) is amended by striking $1,000,000,000 for fiscal year 2017 and all that follows through 2020 and inserting $10,000,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2035, to remain available until expended.

paragraph.text: The heading of part B of title IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 7171 et seq.) is amended by striking 21ST CENTURY.
The table of contents of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 6301 et seq.) is amended, in the item relating to part B of title IV, by striking 21st Century.
Section 11(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by striking 21 and inserting 22.
The amendment made by this subsection shall apply to taxable years beginning after the date of the enactment of this Act.
